_comment: Open vocabularies seeded into lookup tables at init (INSERT OR IGNORE, never destructive). Add a code/label here and restart to surface a new option in the web UI — no schema change, no code change (§12.4). Each top-level key is a lookup-table name; its value is a list of {code, label}. Override the file location with the CATALOGUE_VOCAB env var.
_comment_exclusions: Files kept OUT of the catalogue (skipped at sweep/ingest AND removable from already-ingested data via `python -m catalogue.cli.exclude_purge`). Each rule = {field, op, value}: field = 'path' | 'title' | 'any'; op = 'starts_with' | 'ends_with' | 'contains'; matched case-insensitively. A book is excluded if ANY rule matches. See catalogue/domain/skip.py.
_comment_features: Feature flags gating experimental surfaces (nav link + web route + CLI all check the same switch). Absent = OFF. `multi_work_detection` = the multi-work segmentation review (low-quality; off by default). See catalogue/domain/features.py.
_comment_library_root: INSTALL-SPECIFIC — set to YOUR on-disk root of the library tree (the books folder); left empty in the shipped file. `<root>/A/B` yields subject `A/B`. Drives subject derivation (subject_backfill --read-subject-from-dir-structure) AND relative file-path display in review panes (rel_path), matched as a substring. Configure via the web Settings UI (persists here) or edit directly. See catalogue/domain/features.py + subjects.subject_root.
_comment_local_llm: The local (Ollama) model used for glosses/segmentation. Change `model` to swap models (e.g. 'gemma3:12b', 'qwen3:14b') and `base_url` to point at a different OpenAI-compatible server — no code change. See catalogue/domain/llm.py (local_llm_config).
_comment_external_llm: The cloud model used alongside the local one (e.g. for Tibetan/Sanskrit glosses). Set `provider` to 'claude' or 'gemini'. Keys come from env (claude: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or api_key.txt; gemini: GOOGLE_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY, e.g. exported in .zshrc). Override a provider's model/base_url under `providers`. See catalogue/domain/llm.py (external_llm_config).
_comment_tradition: Controlled vocabulary of Buddhist traditions (the tradition-classifier target + the editable tradition field on person/work/edition). Seeded into the `tradition` table by name (INSERT OR IGNORE, never destructive) at init — add a name here and restart to surface it everywhere, no schema/code change. Medium granularity: the four Tibetan schools + sub-lineages present in the subject tree, plus two scope labels ('Common' = pan-Buddhist/Indian-source, 'Rimé' = non-sectarian). The first entry is the library default used when an author has no lineage.
_comment_honorifics: Underscore-prefixed keys are CONFIG (not DB lookup tables; db.load_vocab skips them). _honorific / _office drive author-name MATCHING only (never storage) — see catalogue/honorifics.py. Edit here to extend; restart (or honorifics.reload()) to pick up changes. When present these REPLACE the built-in defaults, so keep the full list.
_comment_organization: Name substrings that mark a contributor as an ORGANIZATION (a translation group/committee/press), not a person — so it is kept off the person authority-match worklist. Fold-keyed substring match (case/diacritic-insensitive). See catalogue/names.is_organization_name.
_comment_translit: Equivalence groups of Tibetan/Sanskrit transliteration variants that fold_key cannot collapse. These are phonetic English spelling variants, not diacritic-only variants and not Wylie-only forms. Used ONLY to widen author-name MATCHING queries under --person-resolution-extensions; never used for storage. Conservative list; extend only when the corpus shows real English usage.
_comment_library_roots: INSTALL-SPECIFIC — YOUR scan roots (empty in the shipped file). Each: {id, path, derive_subject}. Add via the web Settings UI (persists here) or edit directly. Example: {"id": 1, "path": "/path/to/Books", "derive_subject": false}.
_comment_inbox_dirs: INSTALL-SPECIFIC — YOUR inbox/drop folders for new scans (empty in the shipped file). Configure via the web Settings UI or edit directly.
